right-join.label: RIGHT JOIN
right-join.summary: Include all rows from the right table and matching rows from the left.

right-join-intro.title: What is a RIGHT JOIN?
right-join-intro.bodyMarkdown: A **RIGHT JOIN** in SQL returns all rows from the right table and the matched rows from the left table. If there is no match, the result is NULL on the side of the left table. This is useful when you want to keep all records from the right table, regardless of whether they have related records in the left table.

right-join-syntax.title: RIGHT JOIN Syntax
right-join-syntax.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for a RIGHT JOIN is:

```sql
SELECT columns
FROM left_table
RIGHT JOIN right_table
  ON left_table.common_column = right_table.common_column;
```

This will include all rows from `right_table`, and any matching rows from `left_table`.

right-join-example.title: Example: RIGHT JOIN in Action
right-join-example.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you have two tables: `products` and `inventory`. You want to list all inventory items, even if some products are missing details. You could write:

```sql
SELECT products.name, inventory.quantity
FROM products
RIGHT JOIN inventory
  ON products.id = inventory.product_id;
```

This query ensures all inventory items appear, even if there is no matching product.

right-join-single-choice-1.title: What does a RIGHT JOIN do?
right-join-single-choice-1.prompt: Which statement best describes the result of a RIGHT JOIN?

options.a: Returns all rows from the right table and matching rows from the left table.
options.b: Returns only rows that exist in both tables.
options.c: Returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right table.
options.d: Returns only unmatched rows from both tables.

right-join-multi-choice-1.title: When to use RIGHT JOIN?
right-join-multi-choice-1.prompt: Select all situations where a RIGHT JOIN is appropriate.

options.a: You want to keep all records from the right table, even if there are no matches in the left table.
options.b: You only want rows that appear in both tables.
options.c: You want to find all unmatched rows from both tables.
options.d: You want to include all rows from the right table, with NULLs for missing matches in the left table.

right-join-drag-reorder-1.title: Order the steps for a RIGHT JOIN
right-join-drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the steps to perform a RIGHT JOIN in SQL.

tokens.t2: Specify FROM and the left table
tokens.t3: Add RIGHT JOIN and the right table
tokens.t4: Write ON and the join condition

right-join-code-input-1.title: Practice: RIGHT JOIN Query
right-join-code-input-1.prompt: Using the `inventory_ops` dataset, write a SQL query to list all inventory items and their product names. Include all inventory items, even if there is no matching product.
